title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption:
Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual

This stacked horizontal bar chart displays the 2023 distribution of self-identified religious and spiritual affiliations across 25 countries. The horizontal axis measures the share of the population from 0% to 100%, while the vertical axis lists individual nations. The legend categorizes responses into five groups: "Religious" (dark blue), "Both religious and spiritual" (light teal), "Spiritual" (dark green), "Neither religious nor spiritual" (pink), and "Did not answer/'I don't know'" (grey).

India exhibits the largest dark blue segment, indicating the highest share of people identifying strictly as "Religious," extending past the 60% mark. Nigeria and Turkey also show substantial dark blue sections, reaching approximately 55% and 50% respectively. In contrast, Japan, Australia, and Sweden display very small dark blue segments, each falling below the 10% threshold. The light teal category, representing those who identify as "Both religious and spiritual," is prominent in India, Nigeria, and the Philippines, where it occupies a significant portion of the bar following the initial blue segment. The dark green "Spiritual" category dominates the bars for the United States and Australia, stretching roughly from the 40% mark to the 75% mark in the US. Conversely, the pink "Neither religious nor spiritual" category comprises the majority of the bar for Japan, Sweden, and the United Kingdom, often extending from the 30% or 40% mark to nearly 100%. Spain and Argentina show a similar pattern with large pink sections. Grey segments representing non-responses appear consistently but minimally at the far right end of most bars, generally occupying less than 5% of the total width. The chart visually contrasts nations with high religious identification against those where non-religious or purely spiritual identities are more prevalent.
